1.name: Hydrogen
1.description: The lightest and most abundant element in the universe.

2.name: Helium
2.description: A colorless, odorless, non-toxic, inert gas.

3.name: Lithium
3.description: The lightest metal and the least dense solid element.

4.name: Beryllium
4.description: A strong, lightweight, and brittle alkaline earth metal.

5.name: Boron
5.description: A metalloid used in rocket fuel igniters.

6.name: Carbon
6.description: The building block of all known life on Earth.

7.name: Nitrogen
7.description: Makes up 78% of the Earth's atmosphere.

8.name: Oxygen
8.description: A highly reactive nonmetal and oxidizing agent.

9.name: Fluorine
9.description: The most electronegative and reactive of all elements.

10.name: Neon
10.description: Famous for its use in bright, glowing signs.

11.name: Sodium
11.description: A soft, silvery-white, highly reactive metal.

12.name: Magnesium
12.description: A shiny gray metal that burns with a brilliant white light.

13.name: Aluminum
13.description: The most abundant metal in the Earth's crust.

14.name: Silicon
14.description: A semiconductor that is the basis for all modern computer chips.

15.name: Phosphorus
15.description: Essential for life, found in DNA and cell membranes.

16.name: Sulfur
16.description: A bright yellow nonmetal often associated with hot springs.

17.name: Chlorine
17.description: A yellow-green gas used to keep swimming pools clean.

18.name: Argon
18.description: An inert gas often used in high-quality double-pane windows.

19.name: Potassium
19.description: A highly reactive metal crucial for nerve and muscle function.

20.name: Calcium
20.description: A silver-gray metal essential for bone and dental health.

22.name: Titanium
22.description: Strong, low-density, and highly corrosion-resistant metal.

23.name: Vanadium
23.description: A hard, silvery-grey, malleable transition metal.

24.name: Chromium
24.description: Famous for its use in stainless steel and chrome plating.

25.name: Manganese
25.description: Essential for steelmaking and found in many minerals.

26.name: Iron
26.description: The most common element on Earth by mass, forming much of the core.

27.name: Cobalt
27.description: A hard, lustrous, silver-grey metal used in high-strength alloys.

28.name: Nickel
28.description: Used extensively in coins, batteries, and stainless steel.

29.name: Copper
29.description: Highly conductive metal used in electrical wiring.

30.name: Zinc
30.description: Used to galvanize steel and essential for biological health.

31.name: Gallium
31.description: A soft, silvery metal that can literally melt in your hand.

32.name: Germanium
32.description: A lustrous, hard, grayish-white metalloid used in fiber optics.

33.name: Arsenic
33.description: A famous metalloid known for its toxicity and use in alloys.

34.name: Selenium
34.description: Essential for health in trace amounts, but toxic in large doses.

35.name: Bromine
35.description: The only nonmetallic element that is a liquid at room temperature.

36.name: Krypton
36.description: A colorless, odorless noble gas used in high-speed photography.

37.name: Rubidium
37.description: A very soft, silvery-white metal that reacts violently with water.

38.name: Strontium
38.description: Used in fireworks to produce a brilliant red color.

39.name: Yttrium
39.description: A silvery-metallic transition metal often used in LEDs.

40.name: Zirconium
40.description: Highly resistant to corrosion; used in nuclear reactors.

41.name: Niobium
41.description: Used in alloys, tools, and superconducting magnets.

42.name: Molybdenum
42.description: A silvery-white metal with a very high melting point.

43.name: Technetium
43.description: The lightest element whose isotopes are all radioactive.

44.name: Ruthenium
44.description: A rare transition metal used in wear-resistant electrical contacts.

45.name: Rhodium
45.description: Extremely rare and valuable; used as a catalyst in cars.

46.name: Palladium
46.description: Used in electronics and the production of white gold.

47.name: Silver
47.description: The highest electrical and thermal conductivity of all metals.

48.name: Cadmium
48.description: Used in rechargeable batteries and solar panels.

49.name: Indium
49.description: A soft metal used in touchscreens and flat-panel displays.

50.name: Tin
50.description: Known for its use in cans and soldering alloys.
